parentQuestion: Which type of wave predominates during stage 1 NREM sleep?

scaffolds.q: Brain activity can be measured using electrical recordings called EEG.
scaffolds.explain: Electroencephalography (EEG) records electrical activity of the brain through electrodes on the scalp. Different brain states produce different characteristic wave patterns — rhythmic voltage oscillations reflecting synchronized neural activity.

scaffolds.q: Sleep is divided into multiple stages with different brain activity patterns.
scaffolds.explain: Sleep cycles through stages: light sleep (stages 1-2), deep sleep (stage 3), and REM sleep. Each stage has distinctive EEG patterns. Sleep isn't a uniform state — it's a structured progression through different neural configurations.
